data.title: Phoenix Motor regains Nasdaq compliance
data.description:
• Phoenix Motor (NASDAQ:PEV) on Friday said it has received a Nasdaq confirmation that the company has regained compliance with Nasdaq's listing rule, requiring timely filing of periodic financial reports with the Securities and Exchange Commission.
  • Following the company's filing of its Form 10-Q for the period ended September 30, 2024, on February 12, 2025, Nasdaq has confirmed that the company has now met the requirements for continued listing. 
  • PEV -23.69% premarket to $0.38.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Ameren adds $5B to planned capex to 2030 to meet heightened energy demand
data.description:
Ameren (NYSE:AEE) -2.7% pre-market Friday after unveiling plans for an updated five-year, $16.2B capital spending plan for more infrastructure to enhance grid reliability and resiliency, preparing for potentially large load customers for new energy demand.

Ameren Missouri (NYSE:AEE) said the revision to its integrated resource plan is designed to provide for 1.5 GW of expected new energy demand by 2032, with a balanced mix of generation resources to deliver energy to its customers.

The IRP reflects an overall increase of 1.8 GW of capacity by 2030 and 2.3 GW of capacity by 2035; when factoring in updated costs for all planned resources, the IRP represents an additional $5B in capex by 2030 and an additional $7B by 2035.

The company said it plans to build 1,600 MW of natural gas generation resources by 2030, with a total planned addition of 6,100 MW by 2045; an additional 2,700 MW of wind and solar energy by 2030, with a total planned addition of 4,200 MW by 2045; installation of 1,000 MW of battery storage by 2030 and 1,800 MW total by 2045; and planning for 1,500 MW of new nuclear energy generation by 2045.

On Thursday, Ameren (AEE) reported roughly in-line Q4 results, while reaffirming FY 2025 earnings guidance and forecasting 6%-8% EPS compound annual growth rate for 2025-29.

data.title: Dividend Roundup: Meta Platforms, Nike, Ford, Johnson & Johnson, and more
data.description:
This week's dividend activity included increased payouts from Gilead Sciences (GILD) and Meta Platforms (NASDAQ:META) as well as declarations from companies such as Nike (NYSE:NKE) and Unilever (UL).

Additionally, looking towards next week, industry players such as Ford Motor (NYSE:F) and Johnson & Johnson (NYSE:JNJ) will see the ex-dividend dates for their upcoming dividend payments.

See a breakdown below of some of the highlights of this week, along with some upcoming activity on the dividend front.

Dividend Increases

Here are some companies that have announced a dividend increase in the past week:

Gilead Sciences (GILD)

  • Dividend declared: February 11
  • New dividend amount: $0.79
  • Previous amount: $0.7699
  • Increase: 2.6%

CSX (CSX)

  • Dividend declared: February 12
  • New dividend amount: $0.13
  • Previous amount: $0.12
  • Increase: 8.3%

Meta Platforms (NASDAQ:META)

  • Dividend declared: February 13
  • New dividend amount: $0.525
  • Previous amount: $0.50
  • Increase: 5%

Dividend Declarations

Here are some companies that have announced a dividend in the past week:

Dow (DOW)

  • Dividend declared: February 13
  • Dividend amount: $0.70

Nike (NYSE:NKE)

  • Dividend declared: February 13
  • Dividend amount: $0.40

Hyatt Hotels (H)

  • Dividend declared: February 13
  • Dividend amount: $0.15

Unilever (UL)

  • Dividend declared: February 13
  • Dividend amount: $0.13

Upcoming Dividend Calendar

Here are some companies that have an ex-dividend date coming next week:

Ford Motor (NYSE:F)

  • Ex-dividend date: February 18
  • Payout date: March 3

Johnson & Johnson (NYSE:JNJ)

  • Ex-dividend date: February 18
  • Payout date: March 4

Microsoft (MSFT)

  • Ex-dividend date: February 20
  • Payout date: March 30

Yum! Brand! (YUM)

  • Ex-dividend date: February 21
  • Payout date: March 7

data.title: DIRTT announces share repurchase agreement
data.description:
• DIRTT Environmental Solutions (DRT:CA) on Friday said it has entered into a share repurchase agreement with NGEN III, to purchase for cancellation 3.92M common shares of DIRTT currently held by NGEN at a purchase price of $0.80 per NGEN share, which is a discount to the current market price.
  • Upon completion of the share repurchase, DIRTT will have 189.64M common shares outstanding, and NGEN will no longer hold any common shares.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: SoundHound AI, Serve, Nano-X in focus as Nvidia exits stakes; cuts Arm investment
data.description:
SoundHound AI (NASDAQ:SOUN), Serve Robotics (NASDAQ:SERV) and Nano-X (NASDAQ:NNOX) fell sharply in premarket trading on Friday as Nvidia (NASDAQ:NVDA) exited its stakes in the companies, according to its latest 13-F filing.

The Jensen Huang-led chipmaker also cut its stake in British chip design firm Arm Holdings (ARM).

SoundHound AI shares fell 17%, while Serve Robotics and Nano-X lost 37% and 8.5%, respectively.

Nvidia first invested in SoundHound in 2017 when it participated in a $75M funding round to help it grow internationally.

Nvidia disclosed its stakes in SoundHound and Nano-X in February 2024, along with positions in several other companies, including Arm.

In addition, Nvidia reduced its position in Arm by roughly 44% and owned just over 1.1M shares at the date of the filing, Dec. 31, 2024. Arm shares fell 0.9% in premarket trading.

The new 13-F filing indicated Nvidia still owned stakes in Applied Digital (APLD), Nebius (NBIS) (it invested as part of a $700M funding round late last year) and Recursion Pharmaceuticals (RXRX) as of the filing date.

It also indicated Nvidia purchased a new stake in Chinese self-driving startup WeRide (WRD), buying 1.7M shares. Shares of WeRide surged nearly 100% in premarket trading.

data.title: January retail sales slide more than expected
data.description:
January retail sales: -0.9% M/M vs.-0.2% consensus and +0.7% prior (revised from +0.4%), according to data released by the U.S. Census Bureau on Friday.

Core retail sales (excludes motor vehicles and parts): -0.4% M/M vs. +0.3% consensus and +0.7% prior (revised from +0.4%).

Retail sales, excluding autos and gas: -0.5% M/M vs. +0.5% prior (revised from +0.3%).

data.title: Export prices unexpectedly accelerate in January; import prices rise less than forecast
data.description:
U.S. import prices rose 0.3% M/M, falling behind the +0.4% consensus and +0.2% in December (revised from +0.1%), according to Bureau of Labor Statistics data released on Friday.

U.S. export prices: +1.3% vs. +0.3% expected and +0.5% prior (revised from +0.3%).
